Modelling requires a treatment of binary stellar evolution and galactic dynamics

Why binary interactions?

Binary stars are ubiquitous impactful uncertain

The vast majority of massive stars are formed in binary and higher order multiple systems (e.g., Offner et al. 2023) - and many low mass stars too!

Binary interactions can vastly change the evolution (and nucleosynthetic output) of stars

Envelope stripping, accretion, spin-up, common-envelope events, mergers, and more!

Sana et al. 2012

Many parameters are poorly constrained over several orders of magnitude
(e.g. Ivanova+2013, Katsuda+2018, etc.)

Modelling requires a treatment of binary stellar evolution and galactic dynamics

Velocity kicks imparted from:

  • a binary disruption
  • cluster dynamics
  • time-evolution of galactic potential

can change a star's galactic orbit and the region that it enriches
